functions.name: craft_grammar.create.create_grammar_model
documentation:
Create the code for a grammar-aware class compatible with `model_class`.

```
param model_class

A pydantic.BaseModel subclass.
```

functions.name: craft_grammar.create._get_grammar_type_for
documentation:
Get the "grammar" type for `model_type`.

Returns None if we don't know how to "grammify" `model_type`.

functions.name: craft_grammar._processor._handle_else
documentation:
Add else body to current statement.

```
param statement

The currently-active statement. If None it will be
ignored.

param else_body

The body of the else clause to add.

raises GrammarSyntaxError

If there isn't a currently-active
statement.
```

classes.name: craft_grammar._processor.GrammarProcessor

methods.name: craft_grammar._processor.GrammarProcessor.__init__
documentation:
Create a new GrammarProcessor.

```
param checker

callable accepting a single primitive,
returning true if it is valid.

param arch

the architecture the system is on.

param target_arch

the architecture the system is to build for.

param transformer

callable accepting a call stack, single
primitive and arch, and returning a
transformed primitive.
```

methods.name: craft_grammar._processor.GrammarProcessor.process
documentation:
Process grammar and extract desired primitives.

```
param grammar

Unprocessed grammar.

param call_stack

Call stack of statements leading to now.

return

Primitives selected
```
The GrammarProcessor extracts desired primitives from grammar.

methods.name: craft_grammar._processor._StatementCollection.add
documentation:
Add new statement to collection.

```
param statement

New statement.

raises GrammarSyntaxError

If statement is already in collection.
```
Unique collection of statements to run at a later time.

functions.name: craft_grammar._on._extract_on_clause_selectors
documentation:
Extract the list of selectors within an on clause.

```
param str on_statement

The 'on <selector>' part of the 'on' clause.

return

Selectors found within the 'on' clause.
```

For example:
\>>> \_extract_on_clause_selectors('on amd64,i386') == {'amd64', 'i386'}
True

documentation:

```
param on_statement

param body

param processor

GrammarProcessor to use for processing
this statement.

param call_stack

param arch

the architecture the system is on.
```

classes.name: craft_grammar._try.TryStatement

methods.name: craft_grammar._try.TryStatement.__init__
documentation:
Create a TryStatement instance.

```
param body

The body of the clause.

param processor

GrammarProcessor to use for processing
this statement.

param call_stack

Call stack leading to this statement.
```

Process a 'try' statement in the grammar.

For example:
\>>> from snapcraft_legacy import ProjectOptions
\>>> from .\_processor import GrammarProcessor
\>>> def checker(primitive):
... return 'invalid' not in primitive
\>>> options = ProjectOptions()
\>>> processor = GrammarProcessor(None, options, checker)
\>>> clause = TryStatement(body=['invalid'], processor=processor)
\>>> clause.add_else(['valid'])
\>>> clause.process()
{'valid'}

functions.name: craft_grammar._to._extract_to_clause_selectors
documentation:
Extract the list of selectors within a to clause.

```
param to_statement

The 'to <selector>' part of the 'to' clause.

return

Selectors found within the 'to' clause.
```

For example:
\>>> \_extract_to_clause_selectors('to amd64,i386') == {'amd64', 'i386'}
True

variables.name: craft_grammar._statement.CallStack
documentation: CallStack type.

classes.name: craft_grammar._statement.Statement

methods.name: craft_grammar._statement.Statement.__init__
documentation:
Create an Statement instance.

```
param body

The body of the clause.

param processor

GrammarProcessor to use for processing
this statement.

param call_stack

call stack leading to this statement.

param arch

the architecture the system is on.

param check_primitives

whether or not the primitives should be
checked for validity as part of
evaluating the elses.
```

methods.name: craft_grammar._statement.Statement.add_else
documentation:
Add an 'else' clause to the statement.

:param list else_body: The body of an 'else' clause.

The 'else' clauses will be processed in the order they are added.

methods.name: craft_grammar._statement.Statement.process
documentation:
Process this statement.

```
return

Primitives as determined by evaluating the statement or its
else clauses.
```

methods.name: craft_grammar._statement.Statement._process_body
documentation:
Process the main body of this statement.

```
return

Primitives as determined by processing the main body.
```

methods.name: craft_grammar._statement.Statement._process_else
documentation:
Process the else clauses of this statement in order.

```
return

Primitives as determined by processing the else clauses.
```

methods.name: craft_grammar._statement.Statement._validate_primitives
documentation:
Ensure that all primitives are valid.

```
param primitives

Iterable container of primitives.

return

Whether or not all primitives are valid.

rtype

bool
```

methods.name: craft_grammar._statement.Statement._call_stack
documentation:
Return call stack when processing this statement.

:param bool include_self: Whether or not this statement should be
                          included in the stack.

methods.name: craft_grammar._statement.Statement.check
documentation:
Check if a statement main body should be processed.

```
return

True if main body should be processed, False if elses should
be processed.
```

methods.name: craft_grammar._statement.Statement.__eq__
documentation: Return if a statement is equal to another.

methods.name: craft_grammar._statement.Statement.__str__
documentation: Return the string representation of the statement.
Base class for all grammar statements.
